validation_metadata.validation_approach: Biologically-informed simulation based on expected disease-system relationships

dataset_info.name: Wound Healing
dataset_info.description: Human skin wound healing time course

A1.name: Genomic Stability and Repair
A1.biological_relevance: Critical for wound healing - DNA repair during cell proliferation

A2.name: Somatic Maintenance and Identity Preservation
A2.biological_relevance: Essential for wound healing - stem cell activation and differentiation

A3.name: Cellular Homeostasis and Structural Maintenance
A3.biological_relevance: Important for wound healing - cellular stress management

A4.name: Inflammation Resolution and Damage Containment
A4.biological_relevance: Key for wound healing - resolution of inflammatory response

B1.name: Innate Immunity
B1.biological_relevance: Primary response in sepsis - pathogen recognition and initial defense

B2.name: Adaptive Immunity
B2.biological_relevance: Secondary response in sepsis - specific pathogen clearance

B3.name: Immune Regulation and Tolerance
B3.biological_relevance: Critical in sepsis - preventing excessive immune activation

C1.name: Energy Metabolism and Catabolism
C1.biological_relevance: Disrupted in Gaucher disease - altered energy metabolism

C2.name: Biosynthesis and Anabolism
C2.biological_relevance: Affected in Gaucher disease - altered lipid synthesis

C3.name: Detoxification and Metabolic Stress Handling
C3.biological_relevance: Key in Gaucher disease - accumulation of toxic metabolites

D1.name: Neural Regulation and Signal Transmission
D1.biological_relevance: May be affected in neuronopathic Gaucher disease

E1.name: Reproduction
E1.biological_relevance: Generally not primary in acute disease states

E2.name: Development and Reproductive Maturation
E2.biological_relevance: May be relevant in tissue regeneration contexts

A1.name: Genomic Stability and Repair
A1.description: DNA damage sensing/repair, chromatin maintenance, telomere maintenance
A1.biological_relevance: Critical for wound healing - DNA repair during cell proliferation

A2.name: Somatic Maintenance and Identity Preservation
A2.description: Stem cell maintenance, controlled differentiation, cellular identity
A2.biological_relevance: Essential for wound healing - stem cell activation and differentiation

A3.name: Cellular Homeostasis and Structural Maintenance
A3.description: Protein homeostasis, autophagy, organelle quality control
A3.biological_relevance: Important for wound healing - cellular stress management

A4.name: Inflammation Resolution and Damage Containment
A4.description: Inflammation termination, pro-resolving mediators, tissue repair
A4.biological_relevance: Key for wound healing - resolution of inflammatory response

B1.name: Innate Immunity
B1.description: Pattern recognition, immediate defense, inflammatory signaling
B1.biological_relevance: Primary response in sepsis - pathogen recognition and initial defense

B2.name: Adaptive Immunity
B2.description: Antigen-specific responses, immunological memory, T/B cell functions
B2.biological_relevance: Secondary response in sepsis - specific pathogen clearance

B3.name: Immune Regulation and Tolerance
B3.description: Negative feedback, checkpoint regulation, tolerance mechanisms
B3.biological_relevance: Critical in sepsis - preventing excessive immune activation

C1.name: Energy Metabolism and Catabolism
C1.description: Nutrient breakdown, ATP generation, oxidative metabolism
C1.biological_relevance: Disrupted in Gaucher disease - altered energy metabolism

C2.name: Biosynthesis and Anabolism
C2.biological_relevance: Affected in Gaucher disease - altered lipid synthesis

C3.name: Detoxification and Metabolic Stress Handling
C3.biological_relevance: Key in Gaucher disease - accumulation of toxic metabolites

D1.name: Neural Regulation and Signal Transmission
D1.biological_relevance: May be affected in neuronopathic Gaucher disease

D2.name: Endocrine and Autonomic Regulation
D2.description: Hormone signaling, autonomic control, physiological set-points

E1.name: Reproduction
E1.biological_relevance: Generally not primary in acute disease states

E2.name: Development and Reproductive Maturation
E2.biological_relevance: May be relevant in tissue regeneration contexts
